claims.claim_text: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) affects an estimated 10–13% of reproductive-aged women.
claims.evidence_text: PCOS affects an estimated 10–13% of reproductive-aged women.

claims.claim_text: Up to 70% of women with PCOS worldwide do not know they have this condition.
claims.evidence_text: It is estimated that up 70% of women with PCOS worldwide do not know they have this condition.

claims.claim_text: PCOS is the most common cause of anovulation among women globally.
claims.evidence_text: PCOS is the most common cause of anovulation among women globally and a leading cause of infertility.

claims.claim_text: PCOS is a chronic metabolic condition that persists beyond the reproductive years.
claims.evidence_text: PCOS is a chronic metabolic condition that persists beyond the reproductive years.

entities.name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
entities.description: A common hormonal disorder affecting women, characterized by higher than normal androgen levels.
entities.evidence_text: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is a common hormonal disorder in which higher than normal androgen levels lead to irregular menstrual periods, abnormal ovulation, infertility, excess facial or body hair and/or acne in women.

metrics.name: Prevalence of PCOS
metrics.evidence_text: PCOS affects an estimated 10–13% of reproductive-aged women.

metrics.name: Undiagnosed PCOS
metrics.evidence_text: It is estimated that up 70% of women with PCOS worldwide do not know they have this condition.

source_credibility.reason: Source is from the World Health Organization.
